summary.intent: Test the 'New session' feature that spawns a new Claude terminal session from the Electron app UI.
summary.outcome: The feature was found to be already implemented and complete; the assistant traced the full flow from UI button through API to AppleScript-based Terminal spawning without finding issues.

repo: New sessions are spawned via POST /api/sessions/spawn in src/server.ts using AppleScript to open a new Terminal window running `claude '<prompt>'` in the selected cwd
Working directory options for the session modal are populated from fetchRepoStatuses
The Electron app communicates with a local server expected at port 3000 (claude-hook-handler serve --db ~/.claude/hook-handler.db --port 3000)
The new session modal in ActiveSessions.tsx supports Cmd+Enter keyboard shortcut to submit

code.finding: Contains 'New session' button and modal with working directory selector and prompt textarea
Implements POST /api/sessions/spawn endpoint using AppleScript to open Terminal window
Contains spawnSession and fetchRepoStatuses client-side API calls
Session detail screen, reviewed as part of understanding session flow
Embedded terminal component, reviewed as part of session UI investigation

workflow: Both the server and Electron app must be running independently to test the full session spawning flow

friction: Session was exploratory with no specific issue reported — unclear if the feature was actually tested end-to-end or just code-reviewed
open_items: Actual end-to-end test of 'New session' spawning a Terminal window was not confirmed as executed
Several files were modified (TabsContext.tsx, package.json, package-lock.json) but their changes were not explained in the transcript — unclear what they contain
